• ⚡ Mine has an annual production capacity of 9.44 Million Tonnes of thermal coal — to support captive fuel requirements for Birla Corporation's cement operations

ESPIRE HOSPITALITY SIGNS AGREEMENT WITH MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL TO BRING JW MARRIOTT TO KOSI KALAN, MATHURA 🏨✨🤝

• 🗺️ JW Marriott Kosi Kalan Resort & Spa to come up on 14 acres along Delhi-Agra highway in the Braj region — near Mathura (~45 min) & Vrindavan (~50 min); anticipated opening January 2029

• 🏗️ Resort to feature 151 rooms & suites (incl. private pool villas), 3 dining outlets, rooftop pool bar, Spa by JW, MICE & wedding facilities with 950 sq m indoor + 2,395 sq m outdoor event space

• 📈 Espire currently manages 14 hotels & resorts (Six Senses, ZANA, Country Inn brands) and plans to open 15 new properties by end of FY27

🟢 Impact: Positive (Significant brand upgrade — JW Marriott association elevates Espire's luxury portfolio credentials; strategic location on high-traffic Delhi-Agra corridor with strong religious tourism tailwinds; adds a premium asset to the pipeline ahead of aggressive FY27 expansion targets)

ETHANOL BLENDING – INDUSTRY OUTLOOK

• Government accelerating push toward E100 fuel and flex-fuel vehicles
• At 30% blending, ethanol demand estimated at ~15 bn litres (industry commentary)
• Current industry capacity also around ~15 bn litres

• Near-term view suggests adequate to excess capacity in the system
• Higher blending targets expected to absorb surplus over time

• Volume growth likely across ethanol producers as blending increases
• Policy push supports long-term demand visibility for sugar/ethanol players

• Flex-fuel ecosystem development to further strengthen ethanol adoption

Impact: Positive – higher blending targets drive volume growth and improve capacity utilization for ethanol producers, though near-term oversupply may cap pricing upside

VARDHMAN TEXTILES ACQUIRES 31.2% STAKE IN RENEW GREEN (MPR FOUR) FOR CAPTIVE WIND-SOLAR HYBRID POWER 🌬️☀️⚡

• 🤝 Vardhman Textiles signs Share Subscription & Shareholders' Agreement with ReNew Green Energy Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (Promoter) and ReNew Green (MPR Four) Pvt. Ltd. (Power Producer), along with a Captive Power Purchase Agreement — all executed on April 23, 2026

• 🏭 Project — 19 MW AC Wind-Solar Hybrid Power Plant (26.4 MW wind + 15 MW solar capacity) at District Ratlam, Madhya Pradesh; power to be supplied exclusively to Vardhman Textiles under Captive Rules; operated by ReNew Green (MPR Four)

• 💰 Vardhman's equity contribution: ₹24.29 Cr for 31.2% stake in ReNew Green (MPR Four) Pvt. Ltd. (total project capital ~₹77.86 Cr); payment to be made in 3 tranches based on project completion stages; no board nominee appointment by Vardhman

• 📋 ReNew Green (MPR Four) is a SPV incorporated on June 21, 2022 under Companies Act, 2013; yet to commence commercial operations; no regulatory approvals required for acquisition

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategically sound move — secures long-term captive renewable energy supply, supporting energy cost reduction and sustainability goals for a power-intensive textile business; asset-light structure with phased investment limits upfront cash outflow; aligns with India's green energy transition push)

reports.text:
L&T INCORPORATES 'VYOMA.AI LIMITED' AS WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARY FOR DATA CENTRES & AI INFRASTRUCTURE 🤖🏗️⚡

• 🏢 Larsen & Toubro incorporates Vyoma.AI Limited ("VAL") as a 100% wholly owned subsidiary on April 22, 2026; Certificate of Incorporation received from MCA on the same date

• 🎯 Purpose — VAL formed specifically for establishing data centres and AI infrastructure for providing Infrastructure & Technology enabled Services; yet to commence business operations

• 💰 Authorised capital of ₹5,00,000 (5,000 equity shares of ₹100 each); entire share capital subscribed by L&T along with nominee shareholders in cash

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategically significant move — L&T formally enters the high-growth AI infrastructure & data centre space through a dedicated entity; aligns with India's booming digital infrastructure investment cycle and L&T's tech-led diversification ambitions; though early-stage with no operations yet, the structural intent signals long-term capital deployment in a high-demand segment)

reports.text:
MOREPEN LABORATORIES AVAILS ₹30 CR UNSECURED TERM LOAN FROM SHINHAN BANK

• 💵 Finance Committee approves unsecured term loan of ₹30 Cr from Shinhan Bank for general business purposes; agreement executed on April 23, 2026

• 📊 Loan terms — Interest at Repo Rate (5.25%) + Spread (2.35%) = 7.60% p.a.; Tenure: 36 months; Repayment via 10 equal quarterly instalments with a 6-month moratorium

• 🔐 Security: No asset collateral — facility backed solely by personal guarantee of Mr. Sushil Suri, Promoter, Chairman & MD

• 📉 Existing debt context — Total outstanding loan exposure (ex. this loan) stands at ₹182.62 Cr; additionally, ₹20.45 Cr already outstanding with Shinhan Bank, making cumulative Shinhan Bank exposure ~₹50.45 Cr post this drawdown

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Mildly Negative (Routine working capital/business funding; however, unsecured nature backed only by promoter guarantee and rising total debt of ₹212+ Cr warrants monitoring; 7.60% floating rate tied to repo adds interest rate risk)

reports.text:
BERNSTEIN INDIA STRATEGY NOTE – HARD QUESTIONS FOR POLICY ⚠️

EMPLOYMENT RISK  
• IT/BPO jobs under GenAI threat  
• 10–15 Mn workforce exposed  
• Manufacturing absorption limited  
• Quality jobs vs gig work debate  

AGRICULTURE CHALLENGE  
• 42–45% workforce, ~16% GDP  
• Small landholdings, monsoon risk  
• Subsidy-heavy model persists  
• Need logistics, income transfer shift  

AI STRATEGY GAP  
• Risk: AI consumer, not creator  
• No frontier AI models  
• Data value flowing abroad  
• Need domestic models, compute  

MANUFACTURING REALITY  
• GDP share stuck ~16–17%  
• PLI momentum, but shallow depth  
• Import dependence (e.g., batteries)  
• Need early bets: robotics, materials  

FISCAL TRADE-OFF  
• Cash transfers ₹1.7–2.5T  
• ~0.5% of GDP  
• Crowding out capex risk  
• Consumption vs investment debate  

R&D CONSTRAINT  
• Spend ~0.6–0.7% of GDP  
• Low for deep-tech ambitions  
• Talent pipeline concerns  
• Funding gap in innovation  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Structural issues, not cyclical  
• Policy choices critical now  
• Window open, but narrowing  

Impact: Long-term structural policy debate

GOLDMAN SACHS – OIL MARKET OUTLOOK

• Goldman Sachs expects capex to return to the oil sector
• Peak oil demand timeline shifting from ~2020 to late 2040s

• Brent crude forecast at ~$80/bbl by year-end
• In case of ~30-day Strait of Hormuz disruption, prices could rise to $90–100/bbl

• Downstream pressure expected on diesel, jet fuel, and naphtha cracks
• Refining margins likely to remain under stress
• Naphtha highlighted as key petrochemical feedstock

• ~45% of global naphtha exports originate from West Asia
• Supply disruptions could tighten feedstock availability

• Higher crude + weaker cracks = margin squeeze for refiners
• Petrochemical companies may face input cost inflation

Impact: Mixed – positive for upstream oil producers due to higher crude prices, but negative for refiners and petrochemical players due to margin pressure

BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS — BOARD APPROVES ₹450 CR PREFERENTIAL ISSUE, ₹200 CR NCD, PLA CAPEX REVISION & NEW GYPSUM PLANT 🏭💰✅

• 💰 Preferential Issue of ~93.17 lakh equity shares at ₹483/share aggregating ₹450 Cr to 11 allottees — promoters (Vivek Saraogi, Sumedha Saraogi, Meenakshi Mercantiles) and non-promoters including Tata Small Cap Fund, 360 One Pipe Fund, Alchemy & ICICI Prudential AIFs; EGM called for May 20, 2026

• 📋 NCD issuance of up to ₹200 Cr on private placement basis approved (enabling resolution); terms to be disclosed at time of allotment

• 🏗️ PLA Project (80,000 TPA) capex revised upward by ₹230 Cr to ₹3,080 Cr (from ₹2,850 Cr) due to construction cost inflation, supply chain disruptions and engineering design changes; funded via preferential proceeds, debt and internal accruals

• 🧱 New Lactogypsum Processing Plant approved at Kumbhi, UP — capacity of 76 lakh gypsum boards/annum, capex ₹160 Cr, commercial production targeted by December 2027; to be funded from preferential issue proceeds

🟡 Impact: Mixed (Large equity dilution via preferential issue is a near-term overhang; however, promoter participation signals confidence; PLA capex revision adds uncertainty on project economics; gypsum plant is a smart by-product monetisation move)

Robust HVDC Pipeline in the Offing

According to the National Electricity Plan for Transmission, the CEA aims to scale the current HVDC operational capacity from 33.5 GW to 66.75 GW in FY32.

For this purpose,8-9 projects are being planned at a a total cost of ~ Rs 1,90,000 crore till FY32. Going beyond FY32, additional 6-7 projects are being planned with a Rs 1,00,000 crore investment.

HVDC theme is not a 1-2 year theme. Doesn't mean one should chase at any price. There will be intermittent corrections and that might be the opportunity for those who are not playing the theme.

Information credits: IDBI Capital

reports.text:
UBS on Jubilant Foodworks

Upgrade to Buy from Sell, TP Raised to Rs 600 from Rs 540

Recent de-rating has created a contrarian opportunity (STK down 38% from Jan 25 Peak)

Believe incremental easing of restrictions in commercial LPG supply & a semblance of normalcy will act as key catalysts
Five reasons why Jubilant stands out

1) Domino's is a formidable enterprise that consistently outperformed the QSR sector by delivering positive SSSG for over nine quarters, while peers faced negative SSSG & dwindling average daily sales (ADS) during recent QSR downturn.

2) Jubilant is geared to deliver double-digit structural revenue growth (aided by c5% likefor-like growth), excluding LPG disruption period.

3) Jubilant's business model is shaped for delivery as a mainstay & it consistently tweaks its business model to adapt to new realities, which could unlock 200bps in pre-Ind AS margin expansion over the next three years.

4) Think Popeyes' scale-up is a large value-unlocking opportunity, as Jubilant scales to 500 stores in the next five years while achieving EBITDA profitability.

5) Think Jubilant's valuation is compelling & risk-reward appears favourable.

Downside risks: protracted geopolitical uncertainty, slower-than-expected SSSG and its store network rollout.

₹9 TRILLION TRANSMISSION SUPERCYCLE – STRUCTURAL SHIFT ⚡️

• ₹9T investment by 2032  
• Not capex, structural shift  
• Power story shifting to delivery  

WHAT’S CHANGING  
• Generation largely solved  
• Delivery becoming bottleneck  
• Renewables far from demand  
• Grid infra lagging  

REAL PROBLEM  
• Power exists, not delivered  
• Renewable curtailment rising  
• ~4 GW already impacted  
• Some regions ~50% unused power  

FORCED CAPEX  
• Transformer capacity stretched  
• Execution bottlenecks visible  
• Investment now unavoidable  

WHAT ₹9T BUILDS  
• HVDC transmission corridors  
• Grid modernization, automation  
• Substations, high-voltage transformers  
• Interstate transmission networks  

WHO BENEFITS  

TRANSMISSION PLAYERS  
• Power Grid, Adani Energy  

HVDC & TECH  
• Hitachi Energy, Siemens Energy  
• GE Vernova T&D  

EPC PLAYERS  
• L&T, KEC International  
• Kalpataru Projects, Skipper  
• Transrail, Advait Energy  

TRANSFORMERS & EQUIPMENT  
• CG Power, BHEL  
• TARIL, Shilchar Tech  
• Atlanta Electricals  

CABLES & CONDUCTORS  
• Polycab, KEI Industries  
• Vidya Wires, KSH International  
• APAR Industries  

MEGATRENDS COLLIDING  
• Renewable expansion  
• AI, data center demand  
• Global grid upgrades  

WHAT MARKET MISSES  
• Focus on generation  
• Real constraint is transmission  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Shift: generation → delivery  
• Transmission becomes key enabler  
• Decides power accessibility

COFORGE COMPLETES ENCORA ACQUISITION; ALLOTS 9.38 CR SHARES & RAISES USD 550 MN DEBT 💻🤝✅

• ✅ Encora acquisition (US Holdco & Holdings Limited) formally completed per SSPA dated December 26, 2025

• 📊 9,37,96,508 equity shares allotted at ₹1,815.91/share via share swap to Encora Holdco & AI Altius Parent (Cayman); total consideration ₹17,032 Cr; paid-up capital rises from 33.58 Cr to 42.96 Cr shares

• 💰 USD 550 Mn secured loan facility at 4.6% p.a. (3-year tenor) from JPMorgan, BofA, Citibank, HSBC & BNP Paribas; USD 280 Mn into Encora US Holdco & USD 270 Mn into Encora Holdings

• 👥 Advent PE nominees Shweta Jalan & Atin Hirachand Jain appointed as Additional Non-Executive Directors w.e.f. April 23, 2026

🟢 Impact: Positive (Transformative deal closure; Encora adds digital engineering scale & US market depth; low-cost fixed rate debt well-structured)

DYNAMATIC TECHNOLOGIES' DYNAUTON SYSTEMS SIGNS MoU WITH GERMANY'S AERODATA AG FOR UNMANNED AIRBORNE RECONNAISSANCE SOLUTION 🛩️🇮🇳🇩🇪✅

• 🤝 MoU signed between Aerodata AG (Germany) and Dynauton Systems (division of Dynamatic Technologies) to jointly develop unmanned airborne surveillance & reconnaissance solution for the Indian region

• ✈️ Core platform: Aerodata's AeroForce® X — a MALE UAS with high payload capacity, up to 40 hours endurance, designed for long-duration ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance, Reconnaissance) missions over land and sea; specifically targeted for Himalayas & Indian Ocean Region

• 🔧 Contribution split: Dynauton brings engineering & manufacturing expertise in unmanned systems; Aerodata contributes decades of experience in complex airborne surveillance & reconnaissance system integration

• 🏭 Dynauton Systems established as a deep-tech startup by Dynamatic Technologies in 2023; focuses on unmanned systems & mission-critical technologies for surveillance & security

🟢 Impact: Positive (High-strategic-value Indo-German defence tech partnership; directly aligned with India's Atmanirbhar Bharat & Make-in-India defence push; ISR capability for Himalayas & Indian Ocean is a priority defence requirement; adds significant long-term defence order potential for Dynamatic's Dynauton division)

BofA India Strategy

Reiterating cautiousness on a valuation-led rally

Capital goods stocks are now trading close to upcycle valuations, despite mid-cycle earnings growth

Believe these stocks could correct meaningfully on any potential sell-off in the broader market

Reiterate non-consensus cautious view on capital goods sectors

Estimate +10% capex CAGR over FY26-28 vs. consensus estimate of +15% CAGR

Believe the Street is underestimating the indirect fallout from the Iran conflict across private sector, central government, and state government capex

Slowing capex cycle - see multiple downside risks ahead

Given stretched valuations and risks to earnings growth, remain cautious with 5 of 6 Industrials stocks rated Underperform

Continue to see select opportunities in pockets like Power (Transformers, Wires and Cables), Data Centers (Gensets), Shipbuilding and Defence

reports.text:
UNIMECH AEROSPACE – ACQUISITION

• Signs definitive agreement to acquire Hobel Bellows
• Acquiring 100% stake for ₹450 Cr in an all-cash deal
• Unimech Aerospace to directly acquire 24% stake
• Subsidiary Innomech Aerospace Toolings to acquire remaining 76%

• Hobel Bellows manufactures metallic bellows, flexible tubing, and precision-engineered assemblies
• ~90% revenue export-oriented across UK, USA, Singapore, and China
• Enables expansion into higher-value engineered assemblies and sub-systems
• Enhances wallet share with existing OEM clients

• Deal valued at ~3.6x FY25 sales, broadly in line with industry standards

Impact: Positive – accretive acquisition strengthens export presence, expands product capabilities, and supports move up the value chain

POWERGRID INCORPORATES SPV "MWANGA TRANSMISSION COMPANY LIMITED" IN KENYA FOR INDEPENDENT POWER TRANSMISSION PROJECT 

• 🏢 SPV "Mwanga Transmission Company Limited" incorporated as a Private Limited Company in Kenya (Certificate of Incorporation issued April 21, 2026) — jointly promoted by POWERGRID & Africa50

• 🤝 Established pursuant to Project Agreement with Kenya Electricity Transmission Company Limited (KETRACO) for Kenya Independent Power Transmission Project under PPP framework

• 📊 Equity Structure: POWERGRID — 40% | Africa50 — 60%

• 🌍 Marks POWERGRID's entry into African power transmission infrastructure via the PPP route

🟢 Impact: Positive (First African foray for POWERGRID through a structured PPP; partnership with Africa50 — a pan-African infrastructure investment platform — de-risks execution; expands international footprint beyond domestic transmission business; aligns with India's growing strategic and economic engagement with Africa)

reports.text:
NSE WELCOMES CERC DRAFT REGULATIONS ON POWER MARKET COUPLING; HIGHLIGHTS UNIFORM PRICE DISCOVERY BENEFITS ⚡🏛️📢

• 📋 CERC issued notification (April 17, 2026) on Power Market (Draft Second Amendment) Regulations, 2026 — Grid India to formulate Power Market Coupling Procedure (PMCP) within 6 months

• 🔗 Post market coupling, Grid India (Market Coupling Operator) will aggregate bids from all 3 spot power exchanges (PXIL, IEX & HPX) and run a centralised matching engine to determine a single market-clearing price — aligned with "One Nation, One Grid, One Price"

• 📉 Since NSE's electricity futures launch (July 2025–March 2026), spot electricity prices across exchanges declined ~14%, contributing to higher societal surplus

• ⚠️ Current pricing disparity: NSE's volume-weighted MAVGDDR for April 2026 stands at ₹3,802/MWh vs ₹4,442/MWh under simple average methodology — an unjustified spread of ₹600–700/MWh causing ~₹70 Cr/month (~₹840 Cr/year) higher procurement cost for DISCOMs

UNIMECH AEROSPACE ACQUIRES HOBEL BELLOWS CO. FOR ₹450 CR; EXPANDS INTO METALLIC BELLOWS & ENGINEERED ASSEMBLIES ✈️⚙️✅

• 🏭 Target: Hobel Bellows Co. (Partnership Firm, est. 2007) to be acquired via Hobel Bellows Private Limited (incorporated January 2026); Turnover — FY26: ₹123.74 Cr | FY25: ₹117.25 Cr | FY24: ₹100.99 Cr (consistent ~20% growth)

• 🏗️ Structure: Unimech Aerospace (24%) & WOS Innomech Aerospace Toolings (76%) to jointly acquire 100% of Hobel Bellows Private Limited, which will then acquire 99.99% of Hobel Bellows Co.

• 💰 Total Consideration: ₹450 Cr (Cash) — Equity: ₹8 Cr | Loan: ₹55 Cr | CCDs: ₹387 Cr; Unimech's share: ₹204.92 Cr; Innomech's share: ₹245.08 Cr

• 🔧 Product Synergy: Metallic bellows, expansion joints & flexible tubing assemblies used in aerospace engines, turbomachinery, defence, automotive & industrial exhaust — natural adjacency to Unimech's precision machined components business

• 🚀 Strategic Rationale: Moves Unimech up the value chain from machined components to engineered assemblies; gains access to advanced technologies — hydroforming, thin-wall metal forming, high-temperature alloy processing, precision TIG welding & fatigue/leak testing

• ⏱️ Completion: Within 7 days; No regulatory approvals required; No related party transaction

🟢 Impact: Positive (Transformative acquisition at ~3.6x FY26 revenue; adds high-value engineered assembly capabilities; deepens aerospace & defence value chain positioning; strong revenue trajectory of target adds immediate scale)

reports.text:
Germany cut its growth forecast for this year in half after the US war on Iran triggered a spike in energy prices for industry and households.

 The Economy Ministry in Berlin said German output is set to grow by 0.5% this year, and revised its outlook for next year down to 0.9% from 1.3%.

The ministry forecast that consumer prices will rise by 2.7% this year and 2.8% in 2027, with inflation risks building and raising the prospect of an increase in interest rates from the European Central Bank later this year.

BURNPUR CEMENT LIMITED WINS — NCLT KOLKATA DISMISSES IBC INSOLVENCY PETITION BY MITTAL POLYSACKS ⚖️🏭✅

• 🏛️ NCLT Kolkata Bench vide order dated April 20, 2026 dismissed & disposed of C.P.(IB) No. 37/KB/2022 filed by Mittal Polysacks Pvt. Ltd. under Section 9 of IBC, 2016

• ⚔️ Petitioner claimed unpaid dues of ₹1.25 Crore (principal) + ₹1.00 Crore (interest @18%) = ₹2.25 Crore for supply of polypropylene cement bags during 2016

• ✅ NCLT ruled in favour of Burnpur Cement citing pre-existing genuine dispute — no written agreement, no delivery proof & related party transaction concerns

• 🔍 Key finding: Transactions coincided with previous management tenure; serious financial irregularities discovered post SARFAESI takeover in 2019; entries subsequently reversed before demand notice

• 🚫 Company confirms it is NOT subject to insolvency proceedings & continues as a going concern

• 📋 Petitioner may pursue claim under other applicable laws

🟢 Impact: Strongly Positive (Complete dismissal of insolvency proceedings removes significant legal overhang; business continuity confirmed; protects company from potential reputational & operational disruption)

JASH ENGINEERING – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY

• Targeting PAT margins of ~13–14%

• Aiming for EBITDA margins in the range of ~21–24%

• Increase in depreciation has contributed to higher US revenue visibility

• Business largely insulated from West Asia geopolitical risks due to limited exposure

Info Edge turned ₹3,950 crore into ₹36,850 crore through investments in 111 startups since 2007 - a mind boggling 36% IRR

But, 90% of this value (₹31,500 crore) comes from 2 companies - PB FinTech (₹8,500 crore) & Eternal (₹23,000 crore) which were investments made in 2008 & 2010.

When you remove these outliers, the remaining ₹2,875 crore invested by IE is marked at approx. ₹5,350 crore (a little less than x2)

Mr Bikhchandani wrote an excellent shareholder letter which shared 3 lessons about startup investing:

(1) The Power Law of Returns

(2) Vintage Matters - Children become Adults over time

(3) Staying Power is a Superpower

Lets dive into these lessons

ADANI POWER – NUCLEAR ENTRY STRATEGY ⚛️

• Sets up step-down subsidiary structure  
• Entry into nuclear value chain  
• Focus: generation, transmission, distribution  

MARKET OPPORTUNITY  
• India nuclear target: ~100 GW by 2047  
• Current capacity: ~8–9 GW  
• Base-load, clean energy source  

STRATEGIC TIMING  
• Early entry before policy opens  
• Positioning ahead of private participation  
• Long-term sector bet  

PROJECT NATURE  
• Long gestation: 10–15 years  
• High capex requirement  
• Regulatory complexity involved  

PLAYBOOK REPEAT  
• Early entry strategy  
• Build entities first  
• Scale post policy clarity  
• Similar to ports, renewables, airports  

INDUSTRY MOVES  
• Tata Power exploring nuclear  
• Naveen Jindal Group entering space  
• Private sector interest rising  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Not near-term earnings driver  
• Long-duration structural opportunity  
• Positioning for 20-year energy cycle  

Impact: Positive long-term

Persistent Systems – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY

• FY26 growth at ~17% vs ~18% in FY25 → slight moderation

• FY27 growth expected to be largely organic
• Driven by capability expansion and existing business scaling

• Overall industry deflation estimated at ~2–5%
• Highest AI-led disruption seen in Tech Services segment

• BFSI & Healthcare relatively less impacted due to regulatory barriers

• Incremental AI investments not expected to rise sharply
• Investments to be managed within ~16–17% margin band

• Scope to step up investments based on opportunities; no specific guidance yet

Key Insight
• Growth remains strong but moderating
• AI is both a disruption risk (pricing pressure) and a growth lever

Bottom Line
• Balanced approach: protect margins while investing selectively in AI-led capabilities

One consistent theme emerging from results of manufacturing companies -

Margin pressure

So far from the limited results available, it is  quite clear that metal price inflation is hurting margins of manufacturing companies. In concalls, management of most companies will claim they can easily pass margins and commodity inflation has very little impact on their margins. 
But, in truth, most of these B2B contracts are fixed price in nature with limited scope of price adjustment...

Many manufacturing companies have run up a lot in anticipation, any negative surprise in Q4FY26 results can be brutal !!

FERTILISER SECTOR – POLICY SHIFT & SUPPLY SHOCK ⚠️

• Govt pushing alternatives to chemical fertilisers after ~24.6% output drop in March
• Disruption linked to West Asia tensions impacting supply chains

Policy Action Plan
• Led by Indian Council of Agricultural Research
• Targeting top 100 high-consumption districts (urea, DAP)
• Focus on measurable reduction in chemical fertiliser usage

Reduction Targets
• ~5% reduction in FY27 season
• ~25% reduction by 2030

Why This Crisis Emerged
• Iran conflict disrupted supplies via Strait of Hormuz
• Impacted imports of fertilisers + raw materials

• LNG shortages hit domestic production
→ Qatar (key LNG supplier) declared force majeure
→ Several plants shut → output collapse

Short-Term Govt Response
• Spot LNG purchases at higher cost
• Increased allocation to fertiliser companies

• Subsidy burden expected to rise by ₹20,000–25,000 Cr (estimates)

Kharif Risk
• Disruption ahead of key sowing season (June start)
• Affects crops: rice, pulses, oilseeds, cotton, maize, sugarcane
• Risk of food inflation if supply tightens

Alternatives Being Promoted
• Organic inputs: compost, farmyard manure
• Biofertilisers: microbe-based nutrients
• Water-soluble inputs: nano-urea, micronutrients

Key Insight
• India’s fertiliser chain exposed at 3 levels: imports, LNG dependency, and timing risk
• Policy pivot underway: reduce structural dependence on imports

Bottom Line
• Short term: higher subsidies + cost pressures
• Medium term: shift towards sustainable inputs and supply diversification

INDIA RENEWABLES – GRID BOTTLENECK ⚡️

• ~4 GW renewable power curtailed due to evacuation constraints
• Project timelines stretched from ~24 → ~36 months
• ₹9 lakh Cr transmission capex needed, but execution lagging

Curtailment Hotspots
• Rajasthan: curtailment surged from ~8.5% → ~51.5% (epicentre)
• ~4 GW curtailed between Mar–Aug 2025
• 6–8 GW capacity partially stranded (industry estimates)

• Other states impacted: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu (10–30% curtailment)

Core Issue
• Generation capacity scaling faster than transmission infrastructure
• Structural mismatch: supply growth > grid evacuation readiness

Transmission Build-Out
• ₹9 lakh Cr planned investment (FY23–FY32)
• Target: support ~600 GW capacity by 2032

• Infra additions planned:
→ ~191,000 circuit km transmission lines
→ ~1,270 GVA transformation capacity
→ ~33 GW HVDC lines

Execution Challenges
• Timeline overruns: ~50% (24 → 36 months)
• Avg delays: ~12 months

• Bottlenecks:
→ Right-of-way issues
→ Forest/environment clearances
→ Ground-level execution delays

• Some projects at ~3% progress despite ~28% timeline elapsed

Winners vs Risks
• Beneficiaries (T&D ecosystem):
→ Hitachi Energy India
→ GE Vernova
→ Siemens Energy

• At risk (Renewable IPPs):
→ Adani Green Energy
→ NTPC Green Energy
→ ReNew Energy Global

Key Insight
• Curtailment directly hits PLF, cash flows, and project IRRs
• “Generation ahead of grid” becoming a structural risk

Bottom Line
• India’s renewable growth is outpacing grid readiness
• Transmission execution speed is now the key bottleneck
• Fixing evacuation infra is critical to unlock full sector potential

Key Insight

• AI creating dual impact: pricing pressure now, growth opportunity later

Bottom Line

• Sector narrative shifting from growth → efficiency, AI adoption, and cautious spending cycle

Department of Pharmaceuticals – VALUE CHAIN STUDY INITIATIVE

• Govt to commission end-to-end study of pharma & medical devices logistics
• Consultancy to be appointed to assess domestic + export-import value chain

Objective
• Reduce logistics costs, improve safety, and remove regulatory bottlenecks

Scope – End-to-End Mapping
• Covers entire chain: raw material sourcing → last-mile delivery
• Evaluates transport, storage, handling, and regulatory costs

• Key bottlenecks under focus:
→ Cold-chain gaps
→ Customs delays
→ Connectivity issues

Methodology
• Primary + secondary research approach
• Stakeholder consultations across:
→ Manufacturers
→ Logistics providers
→ Distributors
→ Regulators & healthcare institutions

Cluster Focus
• Major hubs: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, Himachal Pradesh
• Hyderabad identified for priority issue resolution
• Emerging export clusters also to be mapped

Industry View
• Association of Indian Medical Device Industry supports initiative
• Emphasis on:
→ Cold-chain strengthening
→ Multimodal logistics

INDIA RENEWABLES – GRID BOTTLENECK EMERGING ⚡️

The Big Issue
• ~4 GW of renewable power curtailed (not evacuated)
• Project timelines stretched from ~24 months → ~36 months
• ~₹9 lakh Cr transmission capex required, but execution lagging

Curtailment Hotspots

• Rajasthan (epicentre)
→ ~4 GW wind + solar curtailed (Mar–Aug 2025)
→ Curtailment surged from ~8.5% → ~51.5%

• Other states facing 10–30% curtailment
→ Gujarat, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu

• Industry-wide impact: ~6–8 GW partially stranded capacity

Root Cause

• Generation capacity rising faster than transmission infrastructure
• Grid evacuation not keeping pace with renewable additions
• Structural mismatch: supply built > evacuation readiness

Transmission Build-Out (Massive Scale)

• ₹9 lakh Cr investment planned (FY23–FY32)
• Target: support ~600 GW capacity by 2032

• Additions planned:
→ ~191,000 circuit km transmission lines
→ ~1,270 GVA transformation capacity
→ ~33 GW HVDC lines

Execution Challenges

• Timeline overruns: ~24 months → ~36 months (~50% delay)
• Avg delays ~12 months

• Key bottlenecks:
→ Right-of-way issues
→ Forest/environment clearances
→ On-ground execution constraints

• Example: projects with ~3% progress despite ~28% time elapsed

Who Benefits / Who Risks

• Beneficiaries (T&D ecosystem):
→ Hitachi Energy India
→ GE Vernova
→ Siemens Energy

• At Risk (Renewable IPPs):
→ Adani Green Energy
→ NTPC Green Energy
→ ReNew Energy Global

Key Insight

• India’s renewable boom is hitting a grid wall
• Curtailment directly impacts PLFs, cash flows, and IRRs

Bottom Line

• Generation-led growth without matching transmission = stranded assets risk
• ₹9 lakh Cr grid build-out is critical to unlock full renewable potential
• Next phase: execution speed + policy fixes will decide sector returns

5G TURNS 4 IN INDIA — SCALE HIGH, EXPERIENCE MIXED 📶

Rollout Scale — Massive
• India has deployed 5,00,000+ 5G base stations
• Coverage expanded to almost all districts
• One of the fastest 5G rollouts globally

Operator Positioning

• Reliance Jio
→ ~250M+ 5G users (>50% subscriber base)
→ Nationwide Standalone (SA) network — more advanced architecture
→ Clear leader in coverage + scale

• Bharti Airtel
→ Strong urban rollout comparable to Jio
→ Uses Non-Standalone (NSA) network (built on 4G core)
→ Faster/cheaper rollout but limited advanced 5G capabilities

• Vodafone Idea
→ Late entrant; limited to few dozen cities
→ Plans expansion to ~100+ cities
→ Struggling to compete with larger peers

The Real Experience Gap

• Urban areas: strong and stable 5G performance
• Highways/outskirts: patchy coverage, signal drops

• Real-world usage depends on tower proximity, not just “coverage claims”

Regulatory Gap

• Telecom Regulatory Authority of India mandates coverage maps
• But maps use permissive thresholds → don’t reflect actual usability

• Drive tests show wide variation in:
→ Speeds
→ Signal quality

• Current metrics (call drops, uptime) = compliance-based, not experience-based

Key Insight

• India’s 5G rollout = infrastructure success
• But user experience = inconsistent outside dense urban zones

What Next?

• Focus shifts from expansion → quality & consistency
• Unlock SA-led use cases:
→ Enterprise networks
→ Fixed wireless broadband
→ IoT & private networks

Bottom Line

• Built fast, scaled big — now needs to deliver consistent real-world performance
• Next phase of 5G will be defined by experience, not just coverage

**MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY**  
- Focus on balancing operational efficiency with pricing competitiveness  
- Leveraging AI-led productivity improvements to support margins  
- Headcount growth to remain controlled vs revenue growth  
- Impact: Positive — efficiency-driven growth approach  

**FUTURE OUTLOOK & GUIDANCE**  
- FY27 expected to be a growth year, outperforming FY26  
- No explicit numerical guidance, but directional growth confidence remains strong  
- 12-month order backlog provides execution visibility for next year  
- Impact: Positive — improving revenue visibility  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
- EBITDA margin expected to remain stable at ~16%–16.1%  
- Supported by:  
  • Operational efficiencies  
  • AI-driven productivity gains  
  • Pricing discipline in competitive markets  
- Impact: Neutral to Positive — stability over expansion

- AMEA region also likely to shift towards growth (subject to geopolitical risks)  
- Impact: Positive — broad-based recovery potential  

**OPERATING METRICS**  
- Headcount growth slower than revenue growth  
- Expected increase in revenue per employee (productivity gains)  
- Impact: Positive — margin-supportive efficiency  

**ORDER BOOK & EXECUTION**  
- Strong 12-month backlog expected to be executed within FY27  
- Provides better revenue base vs FY26  
- Impact: Positive — higher predictability  

**RISKS & CONCERNS**  
- Continued pricing pressure in competitive IT services market  
- Dependence on macro recovery in key geographies  
- Geopolitical uncertainties impacting AMEA region  
- Impact: Monitorable  

**KEY INSIGHT**  
- Story is shifting from margin expansion to stable margins with productivity-led growth and improving execution visibility  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Gradual growth recovery supported by order backlog and regional improvement  
- Margins expected to remain stable rather than expand significantly  

**IMPACT**  
- Positive — steady execution with improving demand outlook, though limited margin upside

reports.text:
Avendus on Indian Alcoholic Beverages — Sector Initiation
*Premiumization Drives Value-Led Growth | Broad-Based Buy Across Coverage*

**Sector Thesis**
- Alcohol: One of India's most resilient consumption categories
- Demand anchored in habit, social occasions, and rising aspiration
- Strong entry barriers via state regulation, high taxation, controlled distribution
- Premiumization shifting industry from volume story to value-led growth
- Better brands and experiences driving consumer upgrades across segments
- Stronger margin potential as premium mix rises across portfolios

**Key Sector Characteristics**
- Heavy state-level regulation creates natural competitive moats
- Controlled route-to-market structures limit new entrant disruption
- High taxation embeds pricing power for established brand owners
- Category resilience tested across cycles — demand remains sticky

**Ratings & Target Prices**
- United Spirits (USL): Buy | TP ₹1,662
- Radico Khaitan: Buy | TP ₹4,177
- Tilaknagar Industries: Buy | TP ₹550
- Allied Blenders & Distillers: Buy | TP ₹671
- United Breweries: Add | TP ₹1,837

**Avendus Investment Stance**
- Broad-based initiations signal high conviction on sector re-rating
- Premiumization theme central to all five coverage additions
- Four outright Buys; United Breweries initiated with Add
- Value-led growth with margin expansion seen as multi-year theme
- Regulatory moats and brand loyalty underpin long-term investor interest

JPMorgan on Yes Bank — Q4 FY26 Brokerage Note
Underweight Maintained | Valuation Limits Upside Despite Beat

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underweight (UW) maintained
- Target Price: ₹18 per share

**Q4 FY26 Result Snapshot**
- PAT: ₹10.7 billion, +45% YoY, +12% QoQ
- PAT came 9% above JPMorgan estimates — a clear beat
- Opex rose just 2% YoY; came 2% below JPMe — tight cost control
- Credit costs at 28bps; 4bps lower than JPMe

**Key Positives**
- First time RoA hit 1% since bank reconstruction
- Cost-to-Income Ratio (CIR) improved to 63% during the quarter
- Credit costs remained contained at 28bps vs. JPMe of 32bps
- Operating leverage visible with opex growth well below revenue growth

**Key Concerns**
- RoA of 1% supported by 31bps contribution from SR (Stressed Asset) recoveries
- Sustainability of 1% RoA without SR tailwinds remains unproven
- CIR at 63% remains elevated vs. peers; continues to weigh on RoA
- MSME portfolio at risk from supply chain disruptions
- Credit costs likely to inch higher as MSME stress emerges

**Valuation View**
- Stock trading at 1.2x P/B FY27E
- JPM believes current valuation already prices in balance sheet growth
- RoA improvement potential also largely baked into price
- Limited re-rating catalyst visible at current levels

**JPM Investment Stance**
- Beat acknowledged but structural concerns persist
- SR recovery-driven RoA not a sustainable earnings quality signal
- Underweight stance retained; TP ₹18 implies limited upside
- Key monitorable: Visibility on RoA sustainability ex-SR recoveries
Motilal Oswal on Angel One: Maintain buy with target price of ₹400 vs ₹300

Nomura on Guj Gas

Buy TP Rs 390

GSPC-GGL-GSPL amalgamation gets MCA approval

Several positives from amalgamation; share price may rerate

Believe amalgamation & subsequent demerger of transmission business makes good strategic sense along with some immediate economic gains for the merged entity:

1) simpler group structure as it eliminates cross holdings;

2) integrated gas value chain creates scale and efficiency;

3) valuation discount due to conglomerate structure and cross holding goes away;

4) earnings for CGD entity may increase due to reduction in marketing margins currently being accrued to GSPC,

5) accumulated tax losses of INR72bn on GSPC books which can be used by the merged entity to offset future tax liabilities.

Overall, believe merger is EPS-accretive on an immediate basis

Investec on JSW Steel

Buy, TP Rs 1525

JSTL’s proposed BPSL monetization coupled with a strong OCF/FCF profile over FY27-28E points to a materially de-levered balance sheet.

Management’s clear focus on optimized capital structure suggests to us ‘more’ growth capex.

Post Utkal (5MT, Q3FY26 announcement), JSTL has now announced a 6 MT JV with POSCO, likely driven by access to technology and a more efficient operating model

Highlight JSTL’s low-cost brownfield optionality of 18 MT+ (including BPSL), underpinning superior incremental RoCE

📊 HDFC Bank – BROKERAGE View (Post Q4FY26)

• UBS: Buy | TP ₹1175

PAT beat (↓ provisions), loan growth ~12% YoY, LDR ↓ to 94.6%; RoE ~14–15%

• CLSA: O-P | TP ₹1200

Steady quarter; margins ↑3bp QoQ, deposit growth improving

• Bernstein: O-P | TP ₹1150

Stable; ↓ credit costs support earnings, RoA ~1.9%

• Nomura: Buy | TP ₹950

Soft NIMs; PAT beat via lower credit costs, RoA/RoE ~1.8%/14%

• Investec: Buy | TP ₹915

Soft core performance; PAT ↑9% YoY, strong deposit growth, LCR ~113.5%

🔥 **DAVANGERE SUGAR: AGGRESSIVE EXPANSION MODE ON
**
(BSE: 543267 | NSE: DAVANGERE)

DAVANGERE SUGAR is stepping into a high-growth phase backed by capacity expansion and strong funding plans 👇

• **Near full utilization (~96%) → major expansion underway (+85 KLPD)
• ₹127.5 Cr capex to boost distillery scale in next 18 months
• Fundraising plan of up to $100 Mn (~₹800+ Cr) approved**

At the same time, the company has increased its authorized capital to ₹200 Cr, keeping it ready for future growth opportunities and capital needs

Business momentum building with:
Ethanol demand surge, diversification into maize & grain, CO₂ plant addition, and higher crushing targets

Balance sheet actions also in place with promoter support and debt reduction initiatives

⚡ Bottom Line:
Strong demand + expansion + funding visibility = DAVANGERE SUGAR gearing up for sustained growth cycle

UBS on HDFC BK

Buy, TP Rs 1175

Q4 PAT beat driven by lower provisions

Loans grew c12% YoY; LDR down c400bp QoQ to 94.6%

Management expects loan growth momentum to sustain

Expect c14-15% ROE in FY27-28E

CLSA on HDFC BK

O-P, TP Rs 1200

4QFY26 NII and PPOP were largely in line, while PBT was a 4% beat

Would characterise it as a steady performance in a slightly challenging quarter.

Loan growth of 12% YoY was largely in line

Sequential quarterly-average (QA) deposit growth picked up from the past couple of quarters.

LDR (a metric we consider irrelevant, but many investors still find important) declined to 95% from 99% in prior quarter.

Margins were up 3bp QoQ, a decent outcome in this environment when cost of wholesale deposits rose sharply post the Gulf conflict.

HDFCB’s gross slippages have declined YoY on an absolute basis for three consecutive quarters.

JPM on HDFC BK

OW, TP Rs 990

Delivered a steady 4Q26 with PAT broadly in line vs JPMe.

While NII (-4% vs JPMe) and NIMs trailed JPMe on elevated competitive intensity and an adverse product mix, they were offset by lower than expected loan loss provisions.

NII growth for HDFCB has remained volatile, decelerating to 3.2% y/y in 4Q26 vs 4.8%/6.4% in 2Q/3Q, respectively

Expect NII growth to accelerate in FY27/28 on a better-than-expected pick-up in system credit growth and funding cost benefits from a decline in the share of higher cost borrowings.

Decline in average LCR to 114% (vs 116% in 3Q, 110-120% target range) does reduce balance-sheet flexibility to support margins/RoA in FY27

• Hedge policy partially limits upside potential
• Valuation ~6.7x Sep-27E EV/EBITDA caps upside

Impact: Neutral – strong positioning, but limited upside visibility

TATA STEEL – BROKERAGE UPDATE (MACQUARIE)

• Domestic steel price stability supports earnings outlook
• EU sentiment improvement to drive potential upside
• EU business cash flows expected to improve ahead
• Volume upside from Kalinganagar plant ramp-up
• Additional 0.75 MnT EAF capacity supports growth

Impact: Positive – improving demand outlook, volume growth drivers

JSW ENERGY – BROKERAGE UPDATE (JEFFERIES)

• Target price ₹660; rating Buy
• Shift to stable earnings, improved EBITDA predictability
• Merchant capacity reduced to 8% vs 13% YoY
• Confident of achieving 30 GW capacity by 2030
• Net debt/EBITDA to improve with capacity addition
• EBITDA CAGR forecast ~41% over FY25–28E

Impact: Positive – strong capacity growth, improving earnings visibility

JIO FINANCIAL SERVICES – BUSINESS UPDATE (JEFFERIES)

• March quarter results and business update
• Consolidated profit ₹2.7 bn, down 14% YoY
• Net income from operations up 198% YoY
• Lending AUM rises 35% QoQ to ₹257 bn
• JioFinance app users increase to 23 million
• Business building steadily; no financial risk seen
• Exploring life and general insurance venture with Allianz

Impact: Positive – strong growth in lending, digital traction

GODREJ PROPERTIES – BROKERAGE UPDATE (JEFFERIES)

• Target price ₹2,420; rating Buy
• Promoter stake increased by 5%
• Execution, cash flow remain key focus areas
• Targets ~20% RoE by FY28
• Pre-sales growth expected ~20% medium term
• Valuations attractive; FCF positive by FY28

Impact: Positive – strong growth outlook, improving cash flows

Jefferies On Jio Financial Services

BROKERAGE RADAR

March quarter results and business update

Consol profit at Rs 2.7 billion (-14% year-on-year); net income from ops up 198% year-on-year

Lending AUM rose by 35% quarter-on-quarter to Rs 257 billion

JioFinance App unique users rose to 23 million

Building at a steady pace; no risk to covered financials seen

Exploring LI/GI venture with Allianz

Macquarie On Tata Steel

BROKERAGE RADAR

Domestic steel-price stability and EU sentiment improvement to drive upside

Expect Tata's EU business cash flows to improve in coming months

Volume upside expected with Kalinganagar plant ramp-up and 0.75mnt EAF capacity

Nuvama on Paints 🎨

👨‍🎨 Asian Paints to take a second price hike of 3–5% from May 5, in line with expectations. 
🚨Follows a sharp 6–8% increase already implemented across its portfolio in April.
Expects other players to follow the market leader’s lead on pricing

Nuvama on Asian Paints 🎨
Maintain BUY rating
-FY27 seen as a turnaround year, with double-digit revenue growth returning after 3 years.
-Elevated crude inflation may benefit the leader, making it harder for smaller players to compete

Nuvama on Other FMCG Companies 🛢️
-Sharp correction in crude oil could aid a comeback for players like Godrej Consumer, Bajaj Consumer & HUL.
-Maintain view: Consumer companies (paints, edible oils, adhesives, soaps) retain strong pricing power & typically pass on cost inflation over time
